author: y4rr
content: Replaced ferrotorch-jit rustc-shellout JIT with cranelift-jit (#679). Pure-Rust in-process pipeline: LoopIR → cranelift IR → JIT-compiled to mapped executable pages → fn ptr. NO subprocess, NO .so on disk, NO dlopen, NO libloading, NO runtime toolchain dependency. New deps: cranelift-jit/-frontend/-module/-codegen/-native 0.131, libm 0.2 (math intrinsics bound via cranelift symbol resolution to libm::exp/log/sqrt/sin/cos/tanh/pow). Removed deps: libloading. tempfile moved to dev-deps. New API: pub fn compile_loop_ir_kernel(loops, num_inputs, output_len) — takes LoopIR directly, no Rust source string round-trip. New: pub fn jit_supports(loops) predicate filters constructs the cranelift codegen doesn't yet handle (If, modulus, unknown FnCall) so try_jit_compile_cpu_rust falls back to interpreter cleanly. Cranelift codegen handles: loops, scalar arithmetic, indexed loads/stores, all 10 UnaryOpKinds (Neg/Abs/Sqrt/Exp/Log/Tanh/Sigmoid/Relu/Gelu/Silu), powf via libm. Sigmoid+Silu+Gelu lowered as inline cranelift IR using exp/tanh intrinsics. unsafe surface: 2 sites total (transmute code ptr to KernelEntry, invoke kernel_fn) — both with concrete SAFETY comments naming invariants. JitCompiledKernel owns Mutex<JITModule> so executable pages stay mapped for kernel lifetime. Verifications all PASS: cargo check, clippy -D warnings (0), 287/287 jit tests, fmt, workspace build, full workspace test (all crates 0 failures). Latent bug guard from prev session (Add(x,x) duplicate-input bail-to-interpreter) preserved. The #![allow(unsafe_code)] is still module-wide (2 unsafe sites + 2 unsafe impl Send/Sync) — acknowledged narrow attack surface vs prior 7-site rustc-shellout architecture. ADR #663 items 5/8 still open. Tracked: #677 (rustdoc pass), #672/#673/#674/#675/#676/#678/#679 done.
